title: 👑 Elite challenge ⭐⭐⭐⭐: the final gate of 8th year

questions.prompt: Your cousin rides his bike on a busy road with no helmet. Choose the strongest correct way to tell him a helmet is not optional.

options.text: You must to wear a helmet.

questions.explanation: Wearing a helmet on a busy road is a safety necessity, not just advice, so the strongest form is the obligation "must" + base verb: You must wear a helmet ✓. "Should" (b) is only advice; "mustn't" (c) would forbid the helmet; and "must to wear" (d) wrongly adds "to" after the modal.

questions.prompt: Wassim is polite AND quick. Which sentence describes HOW he answered, with both adverbs correct?
questions.explanation: Both words describe HOW he answered, so both must be adverbs of manner: polite → politely, quick → quickly ✓. Option a uses two adjectives, c misspells both (politly / quicky), and d leaves "quick" as an adjective.

questions.prompt: Which sentence combines an opinion and a feeling correctly?

options.text: In my opinion, we should protect animals, and I feel proud when I help them.
In my opinion, we should to protect animals, and I feel proud when I help them.
I am agree we should protect animals, and I feel proudly when I help them.
In my opinion, we should protect animals, and I feel proudly when I help them.

questions.explanation: Everything must be correct: "should protect" (no "to"), "In my opinion" (not "I am agree"), and "feel proud" (an adjective, not "proudly") ✓. Option b adds "to" after should, c uses "I am agree" and "proudly", and d uses the adverb "proudly" after "feel".

questions.prompt: The adjectives are "good", "fast" and "careful". Which line gives ALL three adverbs of manner correctly?
questions.explanation: Two are irregular and one is regular: good → well, fast → fast (same form), careful → carefully ✓. Option a invents "goodly"/"fastly", c keeps the wrong "fastly", and b just repeats the three adjectives.

questions.prompt: Read: "(1) You mustn't drop litter in the sea. (2) I think that friends must help each other. (3) You should to save water at home. (4) Nour looks after her grandmother kindly." Which sentence contains a mistake?
questions.explanation: Sentence 3 has the mistake: this question asks you to spot the faulty one. After the modal "should" the verb takes no "to", so it must be "You should save water at home" ✓, not "should to save". Sentences 1, 2 and 4 correctly use "mustn't drop", "must help" and the adverb "kindly".

questions.prompt: Only ONE of these sentences is completely correct. Which one?

options.text: You shouldn't to argue with your friends, you must forgive them quickly.
You shouldn't argue with your friends; you should forgive them quickly.
You shouldn't argue with your friends; you should forgive them quick.
You shouldn't argue with your friends; you should to forgive them quickly.

questions.explanation: Sentence b is correct on every point: "shouldn't argue" and "should forgive" (base verbs, no "to") plus the adverb "quickly" to say how you forgive ✓. Option a adds "to" after "shouldn't", c leaves "quick" as an adjective, and d adds "to" after "should".
